title: roadmap-business: declare timeline property
description: Adds the timeline property to roadmap-business.schema.json so 4 of 9 failing portfolio instances (EB + Qullqa + JobsiteControl + Viiva) validate. Originates from COLLAPSE-AND-RESUME terminal of spike-2026-06-01-roadmap-business-baseline-drift; spike confirmed data-drifted-from-schema (timeline added 2026-04-12 commit 262d3819, five days after schema locked additionalProperties:false on 2026-04-07). Parent of four child deliverables that pin each venture's roadmap-ci.yml to @v5.

outputs.description: Add timeline property to schemas/roadmap-business.schema.json with items shape that validates the timeline arrays present in all 4 affected ventures (EB + Qullqa + JobsiteControl + Viiva). Pre-work read required: derive union shape from 4 venture instances before authoring schema. If shape does not cleanly union, REFUSE-AND-SURFACE — Option B not viable as-spec'd.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-work shape analysis complete (clean / dirty / hybrid call surfaced before authoring)
timeline property declared with items shape matching union of observed shapes
ajv-cli@5 validates roadmap-business.json instances of all 4 affected ventures against new schema
ajv-cli@5 produces no new failures on previously-passing 2/11 instances
ajv-cli@5 produces same or smaller failure surface on the 5 other-class failures

outputs.description: Cut annotated @v5 tag on schema-registry main pointing at OUT-1 merge commit. Tag message references this deliverable id.
acceptanceCriteria: @v5 points at OUT-1 merge commit on main

preMerge: ajv-cli@5 validates this DELIVERABLE.json instance against deliverable.schema.json v1.0.0
Pre-work shape analysis complete and surfaced for L4 path call
OUT-1 schema change passes meta-schema-check and refs-check
OUT-1 schema validates all 4 affected venture instances
postMerge: Four child deliverables unblocked (pin updates can proceed)

transitions.trigger: Authored from spike COLLAPSE-AND-RESUME terminal (489f1b9 deleted)

notes: Parent of a 5-deliverable family. Single-repo scope per schema; cross-repo coordination via parentDeliverable / childDeliverables references. Pre-work shape analysis (Steps 9-12 of this dispatch) determines whether OUT-1 schema authoring is viable as Option B; DIRTY UNION surfaces back to chat for rescope. Closure of this deliverable family clears 4/11 timeline-class failures; remaining 5 failures (different drift classes) stay on FU-VENTURE-DATA-SCHEMA-DRIFT-1 for Wave 5.
